caloric-conservation-false-law-6ed69c3a.title: Caloric Conservation (False Law)

laplace-s-adiabatic-correction-a8d3a85d.title: Laplace's Adiabatic Correction
laplace-s-adiabatic-correction-a8d3a85d.interpretation: Corrected Newton's speed of sound by accounting for heat release during compression, a success achieved via the caloric model.

heat-engine-work-caloric-analogy-7c34f23.title: Heat Engine Work (Caloric Analogy)
heat-engine-work-caloric-analogy-7c34f23.interpretation: Originally modeled as a fluid 'falling' through a temperature gradient, mimicking the behavior of gravity.

likelihood-equivalence-06a8a37c.title: Likelihood Equivalence
likelihood-equivalence-06a8a37c.interpretation: States that the probability of the data given theory 1 is identical to that of theory 2, leaving the theories indistinguishable by evidence alone.

bayesian-model-selection-83cf7e56.title: Bayesian Model Selection
bayesian-model-selection-83cf7e56.interpretation: Shows that selection between underdetermined theories depends on the prior probability (P(T)), which is often determined by theoretical virtues.

chi-squared-data-fit-f9ff3598.title: Chi-Squared Data Fit
chi-squared-data-fit-f9ff3598.interpretation: A measure of fit where multiple different functional forms (E_i) can achieve the same statistical adequacy.

the-born-rule-e464bedb.title: The Born Rule
the-born-rule-e464bedb.interpretation: Relates the probability density of a result to the square of the wavefunction amplitude at that point.

heisenberg-uncertainty-relation-3e9b6bde.title: Heisenberg Uncertainty Relation
heisenberg-uncertainty-relation-3e9b6bde.interpretation: The fundamental limit on the simultaneous measurability of conjugate variables, viewed as an ontic limit in Copenhagen.

standard-wavefunction-collapse-e3c4bb80.title: Standard Wavefunction Collapse
standard-wavefunction-collapse-e3c4bb80.interpretation: The discontinuous, non-unitary jump from a superposition of states to a single eigenstate upon measurement.

mapping-isomorphism-ec7369f0.title: Mapping Isomorphism
mapping-isomorphism-ec7369f0.interpretation: The formal mapping between a theoretical structure T and the real physical relations R in the manifold.

group-invariance-condition-3fd03f65.title: Group Invariance Condition
group-invariance-condition-3fd03f65.interpretation: Relates physical conservation to the underlying mathematical symmetry group of the energy operator.

relational-identity-osr-acd45e5f.title: Relational Identity (OSR)
relational-identity-osr-acd45e5f.interpretation: Shows that quantum particles have no individual identity beyond their structural exchange symmetry relations.

akaike-information-criterion-aic-68a6824.title: Akaike Information Criterion (AIC)
akaike-information-criterion-aic-68a6824.interpretation: A mathematical virtue-metric that penalizes theories for adding unnecessary parameters (k) while rewarding data fit (L).

logical-consistency-operator-75d7dc2a.title: Logical Consistency Operator
logical-consistency-operator-75d7dc2a.interpretation: A symbolic representation of the ongoing effort to find a mathematically consistent intersection between quantum and gravitational fields.

explanatory-merit-function-e8b4b368.title: Explanatory Merit Function
explanatory-merit-function-e8b4b368.interpretation: Quantifies the 'strength-to-simplicity' ratio, a key virtue in evaluating theoretical power.

lagrangian-parsimony-minimal-coupling-40.title: Lagrangian Parsimony (Minimal Coupling)
lagrangian-parsimony-minimal-coupling-40.interpretation: The simplest allowed form of interaction between a fermion and a scalar field, chosen by simplicity and symmetry constraints.

occam-s-parameter-count-b45fa252.title: Occam's Parameter Count
occam-s-parameter-count-b45fa252.interpretation: Shows how the statistical error increases with the number of free parameters (k), favoring theories that explain more with less.

minimal-action-principle-90ce0d70.title: Minimal Action Principle
minimal-action-principle-90ce0d70.interpretation: The ultimate statement of simplicity in dynamics: nature always 'chooses' the path that extremizes the total action.

the-jacobi-identity-6746f980.title: The Jacobi Identity
the-jacobi-identity-6746f980.interpretation: The essential consistency check for any algebraic representation of physical symmetries and Poisson dynamics.

unitarity-consistency-13b9ff4a.title: Unitarity Consistency
unitarity-consistency-13b9ff4a.interpretation: Ensures the conservation of probability, a mandatory consistency requirement for any valid quantum theory.

microcausality-bound-eca6d265.title: Microcausality Bound
microcausality-bound-eca6d265.interpretation: Ensures that measurements at spacelike-separated events do not interfere, maintaining relativistic consistency.

euler-s-identity-aesthetic-prototype-f2d.title: Euler's Identity (Aesthetic Prototype)
euler-s-identity-aesthetic-prototype-f2d.interpretation: The most famous example of mathematical elegance, linking five fundamental constants in a single compact relation.

einstein-s-elegant-field-law-d8595862.title: Einstein's Elegant Field Law
einstein-s-elegant-field-law-d8595862.interpretation: Condenses the entire 4D dynamics of gravity and matter into a single tensor equality, replacing the Newtonian force law.

gauge-field-elegance-6b1c8c1c.title: Gauge Field Elegance
gauge-field-elegance-6b1c8c1c.interpretation: The kinetic term for gauge fields, whose geometric form is uniquely determined by the requirement of local symmetry.

the-pbr-contradiction-rule-39889d19.title: The PBR Contradiction Rule
the-pbr-contradiction-rule-39889d19.interpretation: The logical condition where a specific measurement outcome is strictly forbidden by the underlying physical state, used to prove psi-ontology.

prep-independence-violation-55154752.title: Prep-Independence Violation
prep-independence-violation-55154752.interpretation: The condition required to bypass the PBR theorem, suggesting that even separate labs are entangled by a common 4D causal structure.

quantum-overlap-measure-7545b5b6.title: Quantum Overlap Measure
quantum-overlap-measure-7545b5b6.interpretation: Calculates the degree of overlap between two epistemic probability distributions over the same underlying physical states.

the-bell-chsh-bound-25b72518.title: The Bell-CHSH Bound
the-bell-chsh-bound-25b72518.interpretation: The maximum possible correlation agreement for any theory obeying the classical postulate of local realism.

quantum-entanglement-unity-61584e96.title: Quantum Entanglement Unity
quantum-entanglement-unity-61584e96.interpretation: The spin-singlet state, whose non-separable form is the algebraic source of all quantum non-locality.

no-communication-identity-4f1d8c92.title: No-Communication Identity
no-communication-identity-4f1d8c92.interpretation: Shows that the local physics at station B is independent of measurement choices at station A, preserving relativistic causality.

success-likelihood-ratio-57a47125.title: Success Likelihood Ratio
success-likelihood-ratio-57a47125.interpretation: Expresses that predictive success (S) is vastly more likely if the theory (T) is true than if it is a random accident.

structural-survival-metric-006c5d63.title: Structural Survival Metric
structural-survival-metric-006c5d63.interpretation: A symbolic representation of structural realism, where the relations (R) of an older theory are preserved within the successor theory.

empirical-adequacy-condition-7a6295ff.title: Empirical Adequacy Condition
empirical-adequacy-condition-7a6295ff.interpretation: A theory is adequate if its predictions match reality for all elements of the set of observables (O).

nozick-s-invariance-metric-6c784774.title: Nozick's Invariance Metric
nozick-s-invariance-metric-6c784774.interpretation: A conceptual measure of objectivity where a fact f is more objective the more it is invariant under the group of transformations Lambda.

complementarity-limit-68ede54a.title: Complementarity Limit
complementarity-limit-68ede54a.interpretation: The generalized uncertainty relation showing that non-commuting operators represent complementary properties.

system-apparatus-entanglement-281b8ddc.title: System-Apparatus Entanglement
system-apparatus-entanglement-281b8ddc.interpretation: Describes the unitary evolution of the system (s) and apparatus (A) into an entangled state before collapse.

boltzmann-entropy-identity-bcb2d134.title: Boltzmann Entropy Identity
boltzmann-entropy-identity-bcb2d134.interpretation: The fundamental bridge between the number of microstates (multiplicity) and the macroscopic entropy.

visual-rotation-angle-f577985a.title: Visual Rotation Angle
visual-rotation-angle-f577985a.interpretation: A simple expression for the apparent rotation angle for an object moving directly past a distant observer.

ehrenfest-circumference-contraction-df0f.title: Ehrenfest Circumference Contraction
ehrenfest-circumference-contraction-df0f.interpretation: Shows the contraction of the circumference relative to the fixed radius in the laboratory frame.

hyperbolic-rotation-boost-406d8594.title: Hyperbolic Rotation (Boost)

amp-u00e8re-maxwell-differential-form-bf.interpretation: Relates the curl of the magnetic field to the free current and the displacement current.

amp-u00e8re-maxwell-integral-form-f5160c.interpretation: Describes the macroscopic circulation of the magnetic field due to enclosed currents and changing electric flux.

displacement-current-density-791bfb4f.interpretation: The effective current density arising from changing electric flux, essential for wave propagation.

cross-product-definition-977782db.interpretation: Yields a vector perpendicular to A and B, with direction given by the right-hand rule unit vector n.

vector-angular-momentum-afe05d2a.interpretation: The angular momentum of a particle with position r and linear momentum p.

irrotational-condition-403d6d33.title: Irrotational Condition

gauss-s-law-for-electric-fields-5eae672d.interpretation: Relates the divergence of the electric field to the local charge density.

gauss-s-law-for-magnetic-fields-4e486725.interpretation: States that the magnetic field has zero divergence, implying no isolated magnetic charges.

faraday-s-law-of-induction-3b4995a8.interpretation: Demonstrates that a changing magnetic field produces a circulating electric field.

4-potential-components-a11750bf.interpretation: Combines the electric scalar potential and magnetic vector potential into a four-vector.

field-strength-tensor-definition-8724f40.title: Field Strength Tensor Definition
field-strength-tensor-definition-8724f40.interpretation: Constructs the gauge-invariant electromagnetic tensor from the derivatives of the 4-potential.

covariant-wave-equation-0c8a0748.title: Covariant Wave Equation
covariant-wave-equation-0c8a0748.interpretation: Maxwell's equations in the Lorenz gauge, utilizing the d'Alembertian operator.

general-emf-integral-4fd7136a.title: General EMF Integral
general-emf-integral-4fd7136a.interpretation: The work done per unit charge by any non-electrostatic force around a closed loop.

coulomb-gauge-condition-0e18071a.interpretation: Constrains the vector potential to be divergence-free (solenoidal).

scalar-poisson-equation-coulomb-gauge-7c.interpretation: The decoupled equation for the scalar potential, identical in form to electrostatics.

vector-potential-wave-equation-d9d90870.title: Vector Potential Wave Equation
vector-potential-wave-equation-d9d90870.interpretation: The wave equation for A, driven only by the transverse component of the current density.

gauss-s-law-for-magnetism-4e486725.title: Gauss's Law for Magnetism
gauss-s-law-for-magnetism-4e486725.interpretation: The fundamental observation that the magnetic field is universally solenoidal.

aharonov-bohm-phase-shift-ff3245ab.interpretation: The quantum phase accumulated by a charge moving along path P in a vector potential.

the-v-a-interaction-vertex-5122e6ba.title: The V-A Interaction Vertex
the-v-a-interaction-vertex-5122e6ba.interpretation: The term \( \gamma^\mu \frac{1 - \gamma^5}{2} \) represents the vector (\( \gamma^\mu \)) minus axial-vector (\( \gamma^\mu \gamma^5 \)) structure. The projection operator \( P_L = (1 - \gamma^5)/2 \) ensures only left-handed chiral spinors participate in the interaction with the \( W \) boson field.

weak-current-coupling-acb42e7a.title: Weak Current Coupling
weak-current-coupling-acb42e7a.interpretation: This current represents the flow of weak charge in the chiral basis. The presence of the left-handed spinor \( \psi_L \) highlights the V-A nature, where only left-handed particles participate in the interaction, leading to parity violation.

the-majorana-condition-5184a5ea.interpretation: The mathematical requirement that a fermion field is identical to its charge-conjugate state.

qcd-transition-scale-0949a70c.interpretation: The energy scale below which the strong force becomes non-perturbative and hadronization occurs.

ampere-maxwell-differential-form-bf5142b.interpretation: Links the curl of the magnetic field to physical current density and the rate of change of the electric field.

cross-product-directionality-9ce4a0d4.interpretation: Defines the magnitude and direction of the cross product, where n is the unit vector determined by the right-hand rule.

biot-savart-directionality-d88454af.interpretation: The fundamental integral relating the current geometry to the resulting magnetic field direction.

faraday-lenz-law-4c32b20c.interpretation: Relates the induced EMF to the rate of change of magnetic flux, where the negative sign encodes Lenz's Law.

lorenz-gauge-condition-9ffad88a.interpretation: A relativistically invariant constraint that decouples the potential equations.
